name: Mirrored
summary: Mirror-uploader service that uploads files to multiple downstream hosts and returns an aggregate download page.

account.benefits: Guest uploads are supported for hosts that allow anonymous uploads. Accounts add file management and the ability to upload into the user's own accounts on supported hosts.

developer.notes: Mirrored publishes official HTTPS API documentation for uploads, account info, and file information.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file types depend on selected downstream hosts; the current homepage lists regular file hosts and a separate group of video-only hosts.

security.notes: The live product and API documentation are served over HTTPS. Mirrored does not position itself as end-to-end encrypted storage.

sources.label: Mirrored homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage verified in a live browser: active local-file and remote-URL upload forms, 750 MB regular upload limit, multiple host choices, and current free service messaging.

sources.label: Mirrored FAQ
sources.notes: Official FAQ covering anonymous uploads, non-hosting behavior, and mirror-service workflow.

sources.label: Mirrored API docs
sources.notes: Official API documentation for authenticated HTTPS uploads and file/account info.

max_file_size.notes: The current official homepage says regular users can upload up to 750 MB per file, while PRO users can upload up to 3 GB per file.

guest_uploads.notes: Guest uploads are supported for hosts that allow anonymous uploads. Accounts add file management and the ability to upload into the user's own accounts on supported hosts.

remote_import.notes: The current official upload page exposes a Remote URL Upload mode.

torrent_import.notes: Current first-party pages expose local-file upload and remote URL upload modes, but do not publish a torrent or magnet import workflow.

stores_files_itself.notes: Official FAQ says Mirrored is not a file-hosting site; mirrored files live on downstream hosts and Mirrored keeps aggregate links.

retention_model.notes: Conditional: Mirrored stores aggregate links and background-mirrors files to downstream hosts, so long-term availability depends on both Mirrored's link database and the selected mirror hosts.

downstream_dependency.notes: Mirrored availability and download behavior depend on the selected downstream hosts and their own rules.

name: MultiUp
summary: Mirror-uploader service that can upload a file once and fan it out to multiple downstream file hosts.

account.benefits: Guest uploads are available from the homepage. Free accounts add queue, file-host selection, and torrent controls, while premium removes ads and expands limits.

developer.notes: MultiUp publishes official API upload docs and an official shell-script upload guide for Linux, making the service automation-friendly even though the workflow is mirror-centric.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file types and delivery behavior ultimately vary by the downstream hosts selected for mirroring.

security.notes: The public pages do not claim end-to-end encryption for mirrored uploads.

sources.label: MultiUp homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage describing uploads to multiple file-hosts, plus upload-by-computer, link, torrent, software, and API flows.

sources.label: MultiUp login and plans page
sources.notes: Official account page describing free and premium account capabilities, including host-selection, queue-size, and torrent-related limits.

sources.label: MultiUp API upload docs
sources.notes: Official API documentation for login, fastest-server lookup, file upload, remote upload, torrent workflows, and link checking.

sources.label: MultiUp upload from softwares page
sources.notes: Official page with a shell upload script for Linux, showing first-party CLI-oriented automation support.

max_file_size.notes: Conditional: limits vary by the selected downstream hosts and upload method. The official homepage, FAQ, and API docs advertise computer, link, torrent, software, and API uploads but do not publish one universal cap.

guest_uploads.notes: Guest uploads are available from the homepage. Free accounts add queue, file-host selection, and torrent controls, while premium removes ads and expands limits.

remote_import.notes: Official pages and API docs describe upload-by-link / remote upload workflows.

torrent_import.notes: Official pages and API docs describe torrent-based mirroring workflows.

stores_files_itself.notes: Persistent storage varies by downstream hosts rather than by a standalone MultiUp storage bucket.

retention_model.notes: Retention varies by the downstream file hosts used for mirroring.

downstream_dependency.notes: Availability and download behavior ultimately depend on the downstream hosts chosen for mirroring.

name: UploadOnAll
summary: Mirror-uploader service that uploads one file to multiple downstream file-hosting mirrors, with a current 300 MB max file size and up to 10 selected mirrors.

account.benefits: The visible upload form supports guest use and asks users to choose downstream mirrors before uploading.

developer.notes: The cited first-party page does not document a public API or CLI workflow.

allowed_file_types.notes: Allowed file behavior depends on the selected downstream mirrors.

security.notes: The current page is served over HTTPS, but UploadOnAll does not position itself as end-to-end encrypted storage.

sources.label: UploadOnAll homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage says maximum file size is 300 MB and users can choose up to 10 mirrors for free file upload.

sources.label: Rentry limited file-host list
sources.notes: Seed list claimed 300 MB for UploadOnAll; official page confirms 300 MB. It was classified as a mirror uploader rather than a direct host.

max_file_size.notes: The official homepage says maximum file size is 300 MB.

guest_uploads.notes: The visible upload form supports guest use.

remote_import.notes: The current first-party page reviewed here only exposes local-file fan-out, not remote URL import.

torrent_import.notes: The current first-party page reviewed here does not expose torrent import.

stores_files_itself.notes: UploadOnAll is a mirror uploader: it sends the file to selected downstream hosts rather than acting as the final file host.

retention_model.notes: Retention depends on the selected downstream file hosts.

downstream_dependency.notes: Availability and download behavior depend on the selected mirror hosts, several of which are older/dead-brand hosts in the current page list.
